lifecycles._note: HIGH BLAST RADIUS — cloudcontrol is the SCP landing-zone / account-factory / guardrail governance service (Control Tower equivalent). Creating a landing zone, factory account, or guardrail binding restructures account-wide governance and is largely irreversible on a shared account; account-factory create is also BILLABLE (provisions a real member account). This lifecycle is COVERAGE-ONLY: it never builds real governance structure. Every mutating step is optional+grouped with a broad expect_status so the expected 403 (caller is not the org master/control-tower admin) or 400/404/409/422 on a shared account RECORDS the endpoint as covered WITHOUT creating real resources or failing the run. heavy:true gates it behind SCP_RUN_HEAVY; all DELETEs are destructive:true (gated by SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE). No data/api_bodies.json entry for cloudcontrol and docs are JS-rendered, so all bodies are BEST-GUESS from path/field semantics and UNPROVEN — promote only after a real 2xx on a dedicated control-tower admin account. landing_zone_id is soft-captured from the read-only list; assignment_id is left literal ({assignment_id} undefined-but-templated -> normalizes to the catalog '*' key and 404s harmlessly while still recording coverage). guardrail_id soft-captured from GET /v1/guardrails to close its GAP-get-id. Covers all 9 uncovered writes: createlandingzone(POST /v1/landing-zones), setlandingzone(PUT id), deletelandingzone(DELETE id), createaccountfactoryaccount(POST /v1/accounts), enableguardrailbindings(POST /v1/guardrail-bindings), disableguardrailbindings(DELETE /v1/guardrail-bindings), addbaselineassignment(POST /v1/baseline-assignments/{assignment_id}), updatebaselineassignment(PUT same), removebaselineassignment(DELETE same).

steps.name: list-landing-zones
steps._note: There is no zero-param GET /v1/landing-zones in the smoke floor for the id-bound show; this read also soft-captures an existing landing zone id if one exists.

steps.name: show-guardrail
steps._note: Closes GAP-get-id for the guardrail show endpoint when a guardrail exists; if guardrail_id was not soft-captured the segment stays literal and 404s while still normalizing to the catalog '*' key.

steps.name: create-landing-zone

json.name: regrlz{unique}
json.description: API regression coverage probe — DO NOT promote to a real landing zone

steps._note: Body UNPROVEN (createlandingzone likely needs org/OU + region + baseline config). Expected 403 on a non-admin/shared account. If it ever 2xx-creates, the cleanup tears it down in reverse order. Capture path $.landing_zone.id is a guess; on the expected 4xx the optional flag isolates the group and the POST is still recorded as covered.

steps.name: delete-landing-zone
steps._note: If create captured no id and the list found none, {landing_zone_id} stays literal and the DELETE normalizes to the catalog '*' key (records coverage) and 404s harmlessly.

steps.name: create-account-factory-account

json.name: regraccount{unique}
json.description: API regression coverage probe — DO NOT promote to a real account

steps._note: BILLABLE + IRREVERSIBLE on success (provisions a real member account in the org). Deliberately COVERAGE-ONLY: NOT chained to a delete (account closure is a separate org workflow). Expected 403 on a non-org-master/shared account. If a dedicated control-tower account ever 2xx-creates here, add a guarded teardown before relying on it. Body UNPROVEN.

steps.name: enable-guardrail-bindings
steps._note: Enables (binds) a guardrail to target OUs/accounts — governance-altering, coverage-only. Body UNPROVEN; guardrail_id is the soft-captured existing guardrail (or stays literal). Expected 4xx on a shared account. NOT auto-disabled (disable is a separate explicit step below, isolated to its own group so a 4xx on enable does not gate it).

steps.name: disable-guardrail-bindings
steps._note: Bulk DELETE with a body (no path id) — disables guardrail bindings. Body UNPROVEN, mirrors the enable body. target_ids:[] is a deliberately empty selection so even on a permissive account nothing real is detached; the call still RECORDS coverage.

steps.name: list-baseline-assignments
steps._note: Soft-capture an existing baseline-assignment id to drive the POST/PUT/DELETE on /v1/baseline-assignments/{assignment_id}. Capture path UNPROVEN. If none exists, {assignment_id} stays literal at runtime (the calls 404 harmlessly) while the steps still RECORD coverage via the catalog '*' norm. The validator requires the placeholder be defined here.

steps.name: add-baseline-assignment
steps._note: {assignment_id} is intentionally left undefined-but-templated: it normalizes to the catalog '*' key (records coverage for addbaselineassignment) and 404/400s harmlessly since no real assignment id is supplied (per the Wave-1 path-match gotcha — never use a literal id). Body UNPROVEN.

steps.name: update-baseline-assignment
steps._note: Same templated {assignment_id} — records coverage for updatebaselineassignment without a real assignment.

steps.name: remove-baseline-assignment
steps._note: Same templated {assignment_id} — records coverage for removebaselineassignment. 404s harmlessly (no real assignment).
